name: Haoming Koo
headline: Applied AI Engineer | Production ML Systems | RAG, Agents & Evals | 8 Live Apps | Ex-Micron | MSc (NUS)
summary: Applied AI engineer in Singapore with 7 years of systems experience. I build production ML systems, RAG workflows, agent tools, evals, observability, and product interfaces. I spent 7 years at Micron across semiconductor manufacturing, cloud, and data platforms. The work supported cross-site programs across four fabs and contributed to USD 600M+ in program outcomes. I now build production ML systems at AI Singapore.
topSkills: GenAI, RAG, LangGraph, Evals & LLM Observability

roles.title: Associate AI Engineer | AI Apprenticeship Programme (AIAP)
roles.description: Full-time AI engineering apprenticeship under AI Singapore's national AI programme. Building and deploying end-to-end ML systems across structured data, vision, and sequence modelling domains.
bullets: Delivered production-ready pipelines using GitLab CI/CD, Docker, and Google Cloud — including backend inference services and frontend UIs
Built vision models for image classification, object detection, and quality checks
Developed sequence modelling systems for time-series prediction
Implemented containerised deployment, model lifecycle management, and MLOps automation
Built 8 live portfolio applications integrating ML models with cloud-hosted dashboards (kooexperience.com)
Expanding into NLP, LLM architectures, LangGraph-style orchestration, observability, and agentic AI workflows

roles.title: Manager, Front End Central Process Integration Engineering
roles.description: Led global AI-enabled transformation and cross-site program delivery across four fabs (Singapore, Boise, Hiroshima, Taichung). Managed a team of 6 direct and up to 9 indirect engineers with a USD 9M operational portfolio.
bullets: Designed and executed a 5-year autonomous conversion roadmap using 3M strategy (Method, Metrics, Mindset) — $832M in opportunities surfaced, $50M+ realised savings, projected 9-figure impact
Unified fragmented process platforms (C3, iConS, PIP) into a single AI-integrated ecosystem — standardised 80% of workflows across 4 countries while preserving 20% site flexibility
Co-led a flagship low-power DRAM yield ramp — maturity pulled in by 7 months on a multi-quarter mobile-DRAM program
Co-led a high-bandwidth-memory yield program supporting Micron's first HBM market entry
Modernised infrastructure from Hadoop to Google Cloud Platform (GCP), improving model deployment speed, traceability, and cost efficiency
Developed ML risk-scoring dashboards using anomaly detection for yield and reliability — preventing USD 100M+ potential losses
Built and deployed GenAI SOP Assistant: prompt packs + retrieval on audited SOPs; 80-90% answer relevance, ~15 min/query deflection, ~2 weeks faster onboarding, scaled to ~3,000 engineers
Designed public-safe enterprise planning workflow patterns with retrieval, governed text-to-SQL, root-cause investigation, validation gates, traceability, human review, and auditable recommendations
Scaled RPA (UiPath/Power Automate) for targeting-lot workflow — 100% adoption; mentored ~30 engineers
Built reliability/observability infrastructure: pipeline health dashboards, canary/shadow runs, rollback runbooks — near-zero ML-pipeline outages
Embedded ESG-linked metrics (GHG, water, energy, waste) and SPC analytics into dashboards for compliance and sustainability governance
Drove SEMulator3D rollout: accelerated validation by 2 weeks, >$10M in time-to-market gains
Conducted red-team UAT simulations, reducing post-deployment issues by 25%
Used spec-driven delivery for ambiguous automation work: write requirements, define acceptance checks, test edge cases, and make exceptions reviewable
Chaired conversion SteerCo with monthly executive updates; managed USD 9M front-end travel/ops portfolio
Mentored cross-regional team across US, Japan, Taiwan, Singapore — promoting Agile delivery, resilience, and stakeholder alignment

roles.title: Senior Process and Equipment Engineer - Uniformity
roles.description: Transitioned from equipment engineering to data-driven automation — developing ML-enabled defect prediction and control systems.
bullets: Built ResNet-50 computer vision model for wafer/chamber misplacement detection via shadow-ring analysis — earned Micron Innovation Award
Scaled AI-based OCR defect detection system fab-wide — 4-5% yield improvement
Reduced scanner wafer rejection to <0.5% and MA excursion impact to <0.1% — earned Micron Culture Champion
Developed anomaly detection and out-of-family (OOF) monitoring with automated MES-integrated alerting
Automated real-time defect analytics pipelines to accelerate root cause resolution
Pioneered novel bevel etch detection using peak colour analysis (vs intensity) — became permanent defense strategy

roles.title: Senior Process and Equipment Engineer - Wet Process
roles.description: Led smart manufacturing initiatives integrating digital twins, ML models, and automation pipelines.
bullets: Built a digital twin of TEL Celesta — ML-guided DoE optimization reduced chemical usage by 60% and saved USD 7M+ (Micron Idea of the Quarter Award)
Saved 40+ hours/week of manual validation through simulation-based process qualification
Deployed real-time sensor networks to enhance process control and sustainability
Qualified >10 alternate chemistries during COVID disruptions — ensuring zero production downtime, mitigating >USD 100M potential losses
Co-authored 7 technical whitepapers on yield analytics, process automation, and cross-site harmonisation
Introduced hypothesis-driven problem solving in conversion workshops with 200+ participants

roles.title: Process and Equipment Engineer - Wet Process
roles.description: Built core engineering and program delivery foundations during the Hiroshima Fab Expansion.
bullets: Achieved USD 7.5M CAPEX savings through smart ramp planning and tool reuse across global fabs
Developed 'MAXout' onboarding framework — boosted team productivity by 20%, later scaled across sites
Aligned Hiroshima ramp plans with Boise and Singapore through remote calibration and parameter normalisation
SAP exposure: material master/product codes, exception handling, and cross-team coordination

experience.company: Tagvance x Maritime & Port Authority of Singapore

roles.title: Capstone Consultant (Geospatial Analytics)
roles.description: NUS MSc capstone in partnership with Tagvance and MPA — digital transformation focused on Industrial IoT and applied ML for maritime safety and enforcement.
bullets: Detected illegal vessel mooring and berth overloading using geospatial analytics with GPS and tidal-corrected clustering (K-means, DBSCAN)
Built scalable ML pipeline on AWS (S3, Lambda) for real-time anomaly detection — reduced false positives by 22%
Developed role-gated enforcement dashboards on AWS with secure access controls
Delivered end-to-end prototype: data ingestion, ML analytics, and visualisation for maritime operations — influencing ~$2M potential annual revenue across four ports
Facilitated stakeholder interviews to tailor technical design to regulatory use cases

roles.title: Scientist Intern
roles.description: R&D in bio-lasing technologies under the mentorship of Dr. Derrick Yong — investigating biological materials as laser gain mediums.
bullets: Engineered and tested laser prototypes integrating biological components for experimental validation
Automated data collection and visualisation processes using MATLAB, improving lab throughput
Standardised experimental protocols, enhancing reproducibility for future scale-up

roles.title: Undergraduate Researcher
roles.description: Hydrogen evolution research under Prof. Jason Yeo — advancing catalyst science for sustainable energy.
bullets: First to identify sulfur atoms as active sites in amorphous MoSx catalysts — novel experimental finding
Executed Operando Raman Spectroscopy to examine catalytic behaviour under real-time reaction conditions
Supported catalyst synthesis and optimisation for next-generation fuel cells

Building the Foundation for an Effective Team

NUS Awards for Study Abroad (NASA) — x2

testimonials.author: Dr. Zhang Linmiao
testimonials.title: Distinguished Member of Technical Staff, Micron Technology
quotes: Excellent communication skills and an aptitude for building consensus among cross-functional and cross-cultural teams
A trusted partner and someone I can rely on in challenging situations
Analytical capabilities, stakeholder management skills, and passion for driving meaningful change

testimonials.author: Vimal Kumar Ramanathan
testimonials.title: Senior Manager, Micron Technology
quotes: Approaches problems with structure, curiosity, and a commitment to long-term improvement
Consistently looks beyond short-term fixes to design sustainable, data-driven solutions
Energy, analytical rigour, and a collaborative spirit — thrives in complex environments that require cross-functional coordination and innovation
